tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_falsy_value_coalescing, test_watch_function_defined, test_watch_exposed_in_js_on_load, test_prop_changes_fire_watchers, test_python_docstring_documents_watch) exclusively use regex/string matching on file contents. test_not_tautological.reason: The tests check for specific patterns that don't exist in the original codebase (watch_entries, fire_watchers, ?? operator, watch_fn prop, watch documentation). A stub/no-op would not add these patterns to the files, so tests would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction says 'Watch callbacks should only fire for changes coming from Python event listeners (backend), not for changes made from JavaScript' — this constraint is not tested. Also, no test verifies that callbacks actually fire after re-render with correct prop values (only checks for queueMicrotask string presence). Error handling is only checked via string 'try'/'catch' presence, not actual behavior.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear grouping comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass sections), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task involves modifying Svelte/Python component code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths (js/html/Index.svelte, js/html/shared/HTML.svelte, gradio/components/html.py), variable names, and commands are consistent and correctly spelled throughout instruction.md and tests.
